results.finalAnswer:
ENTRYPOINT: mcp/stdio/query/verbs/index.js
SUBSYSTEM: mcp/stdio/ingest - language extraction, resolution, sweep, and fingerprinting feed the graph build
SUBSYSTEM: mcp/stdio - stdio-facing MCP runtime layer, freshness orchestration, storage, and query rendering
SUBSYSTEM: . - core aify-project-graph package/root subsystem that ties the indexed project graph together

ENTRYPOINT: mcp/stdio/server.js
SUBSYSTEM: mcp/stdio/ingest - builds the code graph by sweeping files, extracting symbols, and resolving edges
SUBSYSTEM: mcp/stdio/query - exposes the graph_* verbs and renders query results for MCP clients
SUBSYSTEM: mcp/stdio - hosts the stdio MCP runtime, orchestration, freshness checks, and DB wiring

ENTRYPOINT: mcp/stdio/query/verbs/index.js
SUBSYSTEM: mcp/stdio/ingest - language extraction and graph-building pipeline
SUBSYSTEM: mcp/stdio/query - MCP verb layer and compact result rendering
SUBSYSTEM: mcp/stdio/storage - SQLite graph persistence via `openDb` and related DB access

ENTRYPOINT: mcp/stdio/server.js
SUBSYSTEM: mcp/stdio/query - MCP-facing verb layer; `server.js` registers `graph_*` tools here for report, path, impact, file, and onboarding queries
SUBSYSTEM: mcp/stdio/ingest - graph construction pipeline; language configs, extractors, sweeps, framework plugins, and resolver logic turn repo files into nodes and edges
SUBSYSTEM: mcp/stdio/freshness - indexing coordinator; `ensureFresh` decides rebuild vs incremental update, manages manifest/lock/git state, and keeps the graph current

ENTRYPOINT: mcp/stdio/server.js
SUBSYSTEM: mcp/stdio/ingest - parses repo files across supported languages/frameworks and emits graph nodes/edges from source
SUBSYSTEM: mcp/stdio/freshness - manages manifest/git-based freshness checks and orchestrates incremental or full reindexing
SUBSYSTEM: mcp/stdio/query - implements the MCP-facing verbs that read the SQLite graph and return onboarding/impact/path reports

ENTRYPOINT: mcp/stdio/server.js
SUBSYSTEM: mcp/stdio/query - MCP tool surface; `server.js` wires graph verbs here and handlers like `report`, `path`, and `preflight` serve the runtime
SUBSYSTEM: mcp/stdio/ingest - indexing pipeline; tree-sitter language configs, extractors, filesystem sweep, framework plugins, and ref resolution build the graph
SUBSYSTEM: mcp/stdio/storage - SQLite persistence layer; schema plus node/edge/db modules store and query the graph state across sessions
